rationale: Ground truth has no substantive reviewer concerns (just thanks/codex-limit noise), so recall is trivially satisfied by both. Both candidates independently surface the same legitimate edge case (sm_110a/cc 11.x tcgen05 parts not covered by the `==10` gate), which is grounded and plausible given the PR's own description. X goes further with a second, concrete finding (missing (10,1)/sm_101 parametrize case) and phrases both as clear file:line nits with a specific suggested fix (add a code comment, add a test row). Y raises the sm_110 point but hedges it away ('almost certainly fine... I'd lean toward a clean approve'), ending with no concrete ask, and misses the sm_101 test-coverage gap entirely. Both verdicts (COMMENT vs APPROVE) are reasonable for this low-risk PR, but X is more actionable while remaining equally grounded.
